SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.core.observable.publisher

Class AsyncObservablePublisher

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.core.observable.publisher.AsyncObservablePublisher
All Implemented Interfaces:
ObservablePublisher, AutoCloseable
public class AsyncObservablePublisher extends Object implements ObservablePublisher, AutoCloseable
Non-blocking ObservablePublisher decorator: it guarantees that publishing failover metrics can never block or slow the caller's @Failover business call.

The @Failover path only ever does an O(1), allocation-light BlockingQueue.offer(E) onto a bounded queue and returns immediately. A single drain worker (a virtual thread) takes metrics off the queue and forwards them to the wrapped delegate (typically the CompositeObservablePublisher) off the hot path. Whatever the delegates do — Micrometer writes, MDC logging, a remote snapshot push — happens on the drain thread, never the caller's.

Drop-on-full. When the queue is full the metric is dropped and dropped() is incremented; the caller is never back-pressured. This is deliberate: the whole point of this decorator is that observability is invisible to the business call, so a "run on the caller thread" fallback is intentionally not offered here. Loss is made visible via the drop counter (bound by the Micrometer layer as failover.metrics.dropped.total) and a throttled WARN.

Metrics handed to publish(com.societegenerale.failover.core.observable.Metrics) must not be mutated by the caller afterwards — the existing emission sites build a fresh Metrics per event and publish it once, so this already holds.

Disable via failover.observable.async.enabled=false to publish synchronously on the caller thread — used for deterministic assertions in integration tests, mirroring failover.store.async=false.

Author:
Anand Manissery

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    close()
    Stops the drain worker after a best-effort flush of the queue.
    long
    dropped()
    Total metrics dropped because the queue was full.
    void
    publish(Metrics metrics)
    Publishes the given metrics to the underlying sink.
    int
    queueSize()
    Current number of metrics waiting to be drained (for diagnostics / tests).

  • Constructor Details

    • AsyncObservablePublisher

      public AsyncObservablePublisher(ObservablePublisher delegate, int queueCapacity)
      Parameters:
      delegate - the publisher that does the real fan-out (usually the composite); runs on the drain thread
      queueCapacity - bounded queue size; must be > 0. A full queue drops metrics rather than blocking

  • Method Details

    • publish

      public void publish(Metrics metrics)
      Description copied from interface: ObservablePublisher
      Publishes the given metrics to the underlying sink.
      Specified by:
      publish in interface ObservablePublisher
      Parameters:
      metrics - the failover metrics to publish

    • dropped

      public long dropped()
      Total metrics dropped because the queue was full. Bound by the Micrometer layer for visibility.

    • queueSize

      public int queueSize()
      Current number of metrics waiting to be drained (for diagnostics / tests).

    • close

      public void close()
      Stops the drain worker after a best-effort flush of the queue. Idempotent.
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
